text:
In a quiet university town nestled between tall gray hills, lived a shy young man named Leo. He lived in an old, sunlit attic room, where he composed poetry and solved complex equations by night, and read through dusty history books by day.

One crisp autumn morning, while chasing an elusive shadow across the attic floor, Leo found something peculiar—a scrapbook filled with faded pictures. They weren't memories; they were spells. Each one described a beautiful scene, and sometimes a person. Leo couldn't understand why they were so sad. There was a picture of his grandmother, laughing next to a peculiar-looking apple tree. Another showed his old friend Will, jumping rope on the sunlit edge of a pond.

With curious fingers, Leo unfolded a particularly faded page. It was a spell—one he didn't recognize but felt oddly familiar. "Reveal your deepest fear," it read.

Shuddering, he whispered, "What if I lose them? All of them?"

The picture changed.
